---
title: "Week 6 Answers (0331)"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Spice and Wolf (狼與辛香料)

Lawrence and Holo goes from town to town to make profits as a travelling merchant. To decide where bread is relatively expensive or cheap, Lawrence records the quoted market prices of several goods in 24 towns and cities along the route.

The dataset contains:

-   `place`: The name of the town or city.
-   `region`: The broad trading region of the market (`North`, `Central`, or `South`).
-   `market_type`: The type of market (`village`, `market_town`, `church_city`, `port_city`, or `spa_town`).
-   `bread_price`: The quoted bread price in each market.
-   `wheat_price`: The quoted wheat price in each market.
-   `rye_price`: The quoted rye price in each market.
-   `cheese_price`: The quoted cheese price in each market.
-   `butter_price`: The quoted butter price in each market.

Prices are recorded as character strings, and some markets use the currency unit `trenni` while others use `lumione`. The exchange rate is 1 `lumione` = 4 `trenni`.

In this world, rye is often treated as a substitute for wheat, while cheese and butter are commonly sold and consumed together with bread. Hence, Lawrence wants to know how the price of `bread` is related to the prices of `wheat`, `rye`, `cheese`, and `butter`. Follow the instructions below to help Lawrence study these price relationships.

0.  Before you begin, complete the setup:

    -   Open the project you created for the TA Session by clicking on `.Rproj` (if you do not have one, create it).
    -   Open a new script and save it in the `script` folder.
    -   Download the dataset `spice_and_wolf.csv` and save it in the `data` folder. [(right click here)](spice_and_wolf.csv){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
    -   Install and call the packages `tidyverse`, `estimatr`, and `texreg`.
    -   Create an `output` folder inside the project folder if you do not already have one.
    -   (Optional) Remove all objects from the environment using `rm(list = ls())`.

1.  Read `spice_and_wolf.csv` and save it as `spice_and_wolf_raw`. Then create a new dataframe called `spice_and_wolf_tidy` by copying `spice_and_wolf_raw`.

2.  Convert all price columns in `spice_and_wolf_tidy` from mixed currency strings into `numeric` prices measured in `trenni`. (1 `lumione` = 4 `trenni`)

::: {.callout-tip title="Hint. (Try it yourself before you open it!)" collapse="true"}
Check whether the value in price columns contains `"trenni"` or `"lumione"` for each row inside a `for` loop.

-   If it contains `"trenni"`, remove the last 6 characters.
-   If it contains `"lumione"`, remove the last 7 characters and multiply the result by 4.
-   Make sure that all price columns are of `numeric` type.
:::

3.  Create a two-way table of `region` and `market_type`, and add margins to the table.

4.  Fit a linear regression model called `reg_bread_on_wheat` with `bread_price` as the response variable and `wheat_price` as the predictor. Print the regression summary.

5.  Fit a linear regression model called `reg_bread_on_all` with `bread_price` as the response variable and `wheat_price`, `rye_price`, `cheese_price`, and `butter_price` as the predictors. Print the regression summary. Does the coefficient on `wheat_price` change?

6.  Fit a robust linear regression model called `reg_bread_on_all_robust` using the same formula as in Step 5. Print the regression summary. How does the result differ from `reg_bread_on_all`?

7.  Compare `reg_bread_on_wheat`, `reg_bread_on_all`, and `reg_bread_on_all_robust` using `screenreg()`.

### TA Answer Code: Po-Hsien Kang

``` r
library(tidyverse)
library(estimatr)
library(texreg)

spice_and_wolf_raw <- read.csv("data/spice_and_wolf.csv")

spice_and_wolf_tidy <- spice_and_wolf_raw

for(i in 1:nrow(spice_and_wolf_tidy)){
  if(grepl("trenni", spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4])){
    spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8] <- substring(
      spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8], 
      1, 
      nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8]) - 6
      ) %>% as.numeric()
  }
  else{
    spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8] <- (substring(
      spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8],
      1, 
      nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8]) - 7
      ) %>% as.numeric()
      ) * 4
  }
}

price_col <- colnames(spice_and_wolf_tidy)[4:8]
for(col in price_col){
  spice_and_wolf_tidy[[col]] <- as.numeric(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[col]])
}

addmargins(table(spice_and_wolf_tidy$region, spice_and_wolf_tidy$market_type))

reg_bread_on_wheat <- lm(bread_price ~ wheat_price, data = spice_and_wolf_tidy)
summary(reg_bread_on_wheat)

reg_bread_on_all <- lm(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)
summary(reg_bread_on_all)

reg_bread_on_all_robust <- lm_robust(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)
summary(reg_bread_on_all_robust)

screenreg(
  list(reg_bread_on_wheat, reg_bread_on_all, reg_bread_on_all_robust),
  custom.model.names = c("Bread on wheat", "Bread on all goods", "Bread on all goods (Robust)"),
  include.ci = FALSE,
  digit = 3
)
```

### TA Answer Code: Shuhan Chen

``` r
library(tidyverse)
library(estimatr)
library(texreg)
rm(list = ls())

spice_and_wolf_tidy <- spice_and_wolf_raw

for (i in 4:8) {
  spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]] <- ifelse(
    grepl("trenni", spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]]),
    as.numeric(substring(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]], 1, nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]]) - 6)),
    as.numeric(substring(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]], 1, nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[i]]) - 7)) * 4
  )
}

# create table
# =================
spice_table <- table(spice_and_wolf_tidy$region, spice_and_wolf_tidy$market_type)
spice_table <- addmargins(spice_table)

# reg
# =================
reg_bread_on_wheat <- lm(data = spice_and_wolf_tidy,
                         bread_price ~ wheat_price)
summary(reg_bread_on_wheat)


reg_bread_on_all <- lm(data = spice_and_wolf_tidy,
                         bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price)
summary(reg_bread_on_all)
# Yes, 1.21 => 0.77


reg_bread_on_all_robust <- lm_robust(data = spice_and_wolf_tidy,
                          bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price)
summary(reg_bread_on_all_robust)
# Yes, rye p-value 0.03 => 0.09

screenreg(
  list(reg_bread_on_wheat, reg_bread_on_all, reg_bread_on_all_robust),
  custom.model.names =
    c("Model 1: Single",
      "Model 2: Multiple",
      "Model 3: Multiple (Robust SE)"),
  # present standard error
  include.ci = FALSE,
  digit = 3
)
```

### TA Answer Code: Tom

``` r
library(tidyverse)
library(estimatr)
library(texreg)

spice_and_wolf_raw <- read_csv("data/spice_and_wolf.csv")
spice_and_wolf_tidy <- spice_and_wolf_raw

price_cols <- c(
  "bread_price",
  "wheat_price",
  "rye_price",
  "cheese_price",
  "butter_price"
)

convert_to_trenni <- function(x) {
  value_num <- ifelse(
    grepl("lumione", x),
    as.numeric(substring(x, 1, nchar(x) - 8)),
    ifelse(
      grepl("trenni", x),
      as.numeric(substring(x, 1, nchar(x) - 7)), 
      NA
    )
  )
  
  value_trenni <- ifelse(
    grepl("lumione", x),
    value_num * 4,
    ifelse(
      grepl("trenni", x),
      value_num,
      NA
    )
  )
  
  return(value_trenni)
}

for (v in price_cols) {
  spice_and_wolf_tidy[[v]] <- convert_to_trenni(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[v]])
}

region_market_tab <- table(
  spice_and_wolf_tidy$region,
  spice_and_wolf_tidy$market_type
)

row_totals <- rowSums(region_market_tab)
col_totals <- colSums(region_market_tab)
grand_total <- sum(region_market_tab)

region_market_tab_with_total <- rbind(region_market_tab, Total = col_totals)
region_market_tab_with_total <- cbind(
  region_market_tab_with_total,
  Total = c(row_totals, grand_total)
)

region_market_tab_with_total

reg_bread_on_wheat <- lm(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)

summary(reg_bread_on_wheat)

reg_bread_on_all <- lm(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)

summary(reg_bread_on_all)

coef(reg_bread_on_wheat)["wheat_price"]
coef(reg_bread_on_all)["wheat_price"]

reg_bread_on_all_robust <- lm_robust(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)

summary(reg_bread_on_all_robust)

screenreg(
  list(
    reg_bread_on_wheat,
    reg_bread_on_all,
    reg_bread_on_all_robust
  ),
  custom.model.names = c(
    "Bread on Wheat",
    "Bread on All",
    "Bread on All (Robust)"
  ),
  digits = 3
)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false      

rm(list = ls())

library(tidyverse)
library(estimatr)
library(texreg)
library(here)

spice_and_wolf_raw <- read.csv(here("data", "spice_and_wolf.csv"))

spice_and_wolf_tidy <- spice_and_wolf_raw

for(i in 1:nrow(spice_and_wolf_tidy)){
  if(grepl("trenni", spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4])){
    spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8] <- substring(
      spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8], 
      1, 
      nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8]) - 6
      ) %>% as.numeric()
  }
  else{
    spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8] <- (substring(
      spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8],
      1, 
      nchar(spice_and_wolf_tidy[i, 4:8]) - 7
      ) %>% as.numeric()
      ) * 4
  }
}

price_col <- colnames(spice_and_wolf_tidy)[4:8]
for(col in price_col){
  spice_and_wolf_tidy[[col]] <- as.numeric(spice_and_wolf_tidy[[col]])
}


addmargins(table(spice_and_wolf_tidy$region, spice_and_wolf_tidy$market_type))


reg_bread_on_wheat <- lm(bread_price ~ wheat_price, data = spice_and_wolf_tidy)
summary(reg_bread_on_wheat)

reg_bread_on_all <- lm(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)
summary(reg_bread_on_all)

reg_bread_on_all_robust <- lm_robust(
  bread_price ~ wheat_price + rye_price + cheese_price + butter_price,
  data = spice_and_wolf_tidy
)
summary(reg_bread_on_all_robust)

screenreg(
  list(reg_bread_on_wheat, reg_bread_on_all, reg_bread_on_all_robust),
  custom.model.names = c("Bread on wheat", "Bread on all goods", "Bread on all goods (Robust)"),
  include.ci = FALSE,
  digit = 3
)

```

![](spice_and_wolf.gif){width="960" fig-align="center" fig-alt="Animated welcome"}
